claim: Animal-care evidence maps become safer and more useful when each observation is separated from interpretation, species/context metadata, missingness, privacy state, vet-review questions, blocked care directives, and falsification route before export.

missingness.rule: missing information cannot be interpreted as absence of risk or absence of condition

revision_reason: adds animal-care question-prep infrastructure with stricter observation/interpretation separation and blocked directive detection
testability: validator checks required metadata, observation separation, measurable variables, privacy state, safe export routes, blocked directives, and negative controls
falsification_route: claim fails if fixture permits diagnosis, treatment, dosage, emergency triage, unsupported causality, cure certainty, private identifiers, or missingness-as-absence

measurable_variables.name: observation_interpretation_separation_rate
measurable_variables.measurement_context: records with distinct observation and interpretation fields

measurable_variables.name: vet_question_presence_rate
measurable_variables.measurement_context: records with at least one professional-review question

measurable_variables.name: blocked_directive_count
measurable_variables.measurement_context: unsafe care directives blocked per record

records.observation: Caregiver reports a visible eye-status change after travel; timing and professional measurements are incomplete.
records.interpretation_boundary: May be useful as timeline context for a veterinarian; not evidence of cause, diagnosis, treatment need, or urgency level.
records.revision_reason: converted emotionally urgent animal-care note into bounded question-prep record
records.testability: compare observation timestamp, travel window, medication timing log, and professional exam results if available
records.falsification_route: fails as a pattern claim if professional measurements or later records do not align with reported timing

measurable_variables.name: observation_timestamp_precision
measurable_variables.measurement_context: known time, day only, or unknown

measurable_variables.name: visible_change_score
measurable_variables.measurement_context: caregiver photo-review scale for question prep only

professional_review_questions: What measurements or exam observations would distinguish benign variation from a clinically relevant change?
records.next_executable_action: Add timestamped observation row with source boundary and vet-review question.

records.observation: Caregiver reports facial skin changes in multiple cats; household timing and parasite/allergy/infection evidence are incomplete.
records.interpretation_boundary: Supports organizing differentials as questions for professional review; does not choose a cause or recommend an intervention.
records.revision_reason: keeps multi-animal pattern as evidence-map question rather than care directive
records.testability: track affected animals, body locations, onset windows, environmental changes, and professional test results
records.falsification_route: fails as a shared-context pattern if animals have unrelated timing, locations, or professional findings
professional_review_questions: Which tests or observations would separate shared environmental exposure from unrelated individual causes?
records.next_executable_action: Create a deidentified household timeline table with one row per animal and observation window.

records.observation: Caregiver reports behavior change after a medication exposure; dose details, timing precision, concurrent stressors, and exam findings are incomplete.
records.interpretation_boundary: Can be prepared as a timeline question for a veterinarian; not proof of adverse reaction, not medication guidance, and not a future dosing rule.
records.revision_reason: separates temporal association from causal or medication directive language
records.testability: compare administration timestamp, behavior onset, duration, concurrent stressors, and professional interpretation
records.falsification_route: fails as temporal-association evidence if timestamps are incompatible or alternative context fully explains the change
professional_review_questions: What timeline details would help a veterinarian evaluate whether this was exposure-related or context-related?
records.next_executable_action: Convert narrative into exposure/behavior timeline with unknown fields marked explicitly.

negative_controls.unsafe_text: Diagnose the dog with glaucoma worsening and give 5 mg medication tonight.
Missing vet data means the skin problem is absent and no emergency can happen.
This proves the medication caused the reaction and the treatment should be stopped forever.
